rationale: The subject property (blue-tarped hip roof, upper structure) is located in Cape Coral, southwest Florida, where building codes and climate strongly favor low-pitch hip roofs (4:12–6:12). Visual cues support 4:12: the northward eave shadow is short relative to the ~40–45 ft building width (per the 50-ft scale bar), indicating limited vertical rise. The hip roof planes converge gradually and the ridge remains relatively wide in plan view, consistent with a shallow pitch. The neighboring gray-shingled hip roof (lower structure) reinforces the neighborhood norm of low-profile Florida construction. No dormers, steep gable protrusions, or long dramatic shadows suggestive of 6:12+ are present.
